summary: UR003 is an INTEGRATED AGRICULTURAL CALENDAR from Leymebamba (Chachapoyas, Amazonas) -- same site as UR006. 769 cords, 12 colours, deep tree to L5 (L1: 281, L2: 400, L3: 79, L4: 8, L5: 1). RECLASSIFIED 2026-04 from astronomical_journal to agricultural_calendar: 12 of 13 words fit farming vocabulary vs only 5 of 13 under a pure astronomical reading. Key reading: qaqa x7 means 'rocky terrain / terraces' (not 'dark constellation'); kaki means 'corn AND Pleiades' (the Inca doublet where the star cluster signals when to plant the corn); waki = communal labour; llaqa = village. PHYSICAL DAMAGE reported by OKR: 'Una interrupcion por descomposicion del cordon principal; descartamos una cantidad de pendientes por su fragilidad.' Primary cord decomposed, fragile pendants discarded. S/Z direction NOT recorded. Recording INCOMPLETE. KEY STRUCTURAL DISCOVERY: ALL 21 STRING cords are on L2 or L3 -- NONE on L1. The tree is INVERTED compared to juridical khipus: L1 = NUMBERS (primary data), L2/L3 = TEXT LABELS (annotations). Numbers come first; words explain what was counted. Hallmark of an agricultural PRODUCTION REGISTER. 22 STRING at 95 percent dict hit. Centro Mallqui, Leymebamba, Amazonas. Companion of UR006 (June 1473 astronomical observation journal).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  769 cords (L1: 281, L2: 400, L3: 79, L4: 8, L5: 1). Architecture: 0-SEUL but DEEP TREE (L2/L1 = 1.42, max L5).
  12 colors: AB(254), MB(165), W(111), KB(79), RL(63), LC(41), GG(24), ?(14), RM(12), BG(1), MG(1).
  15 clusters. 38 percent empty -- sparse, partially-filled register.
  Provenance: Leymebamba (same site as UR006 dated June 1473).
  Museum: Centro Mallqui, Leymebamba, Amazonas.
  DAMAGED -- OKR (Spanish): 'Una interrupcion por descomposicion del cordon principal; descartamos
  una cantidad de pendientes por su fragilidad.' Primary cord DECOMPOSED, fragile pendants discarded.
  OKR: 'Parece que hay variacion en la direccion de anudar los nudos finales (unos en Z, otros en S).
  Yo no he anotado las direcciones.' S/Z NOT recorded.
  OKR: 'Por falta del tiempo...' Recording INCOMPLETE.
  22 STRING (95 percent dict hits, 21/22), 451 INT, 296 EMPTY.

RECLASSIFICATION FROM ASTRONOMICAL TO AGRICULTURAL (2026-04)
  Vocabulary re-analysis: 12/13 words make sense as farming vocabulary vs 5/13 in pure astronomical.
  Sky/ground polysemy context: kaki is BOTH 'Pleiades' AND 'dried corn' in Inca worldview because
  the Pleiades' heliacal rising signals the corn-planting season. qaqa is BOTH 'dark constellation'
  AND 'rocky terrain' because the same root names the sky object and the ground category that the
  constellation's appearance governs. In an Inca agricultural calendar these doublets point to the
  GROUND register; in an observatory journal (UR006, AS069) they point to the SKY register. UR003's
  deep-tree + numbers-primary + waki + llaqa + chiqa + waqa pattern points to the GROUND.

TREE READING -- KEY DISCOVERY
  ALL 21 STRING cords are on L2 or L3 -- NONE on L1. The tree structure is INVERTED vs juridical khipus:
    L1 = NUMERICAL entry (count, measurement, quantity) <- PRIMARY DATA
      L2 = TEXT LABEL (what is counted) <- annotations identifying the numbers
        L3 = SUB-LABEL (additional specification)
  The NUMBERS are primary. The WORDS are annotations.
  5-level tree depth encoding hypothesis:
    L1 = Production period (281 entries, all INT/EMPTY)
      L2 = Category LABEL (400 entries, 20 STRING: qaqa=terrain, kaki=corn)
        L3 = Detail (79 entries, 1 STRING: qaqa)
          L4 = Sub-detail (8 entries)
            L5 = Individual detail (1 entry)

KEY VOCABULARY (13 distinct, 22 STRING, 95 percent dict hit)
  qaqa x7 -- rocky terrain / terraces (DOMINANT terrain label)
  kaki x2 -- corn / Pleiades (dual-sky-ground doublet; planting signal AND the harvest itself)
  waki x2 -- communal labor (mit'a cooperation)
  chiqa x2 -- field inspection / verification
  paka x1 -- stored harvest / hidden crops
  taqa x1 -- field boundaries / land management
  kaqa x1 -- terrace walls (Aymara)
  waqa x1 -- huaca / sacred site / offerings
  maqa x1 -- harvesting / striking the grain
  maki x1 -- manual labor / fieldwork
  llaqa x1 -- village (which community farms)
  kiki x1 -- self / individual mit'a worker
  qaki x1 -- frost / dry? (uncertain)

COLOR-AS-ROLE
  AB (254, 9 STRING) = GEOGRAPHIC -- terrain labels (qaqa x6, kaqa, kaki, chiqa).
  LC (41,  6 STRING) = RITUAL / LABOR -- ceremonial aspect (qaki, kiki, waki, maki, chiqa).
  GG (24,  3 STRING) = GOVERNANCE -- boundaries, storage, corn tribute (paka, taqa, kaki).
  MB (165, 1 STRING) = HUACA -- waqa (sacred site).
  KB (79,  1 STRING) = ECLIPSE / IMPACT -- maqa.
  LC carries 6/21 words (29 percent) = HIGHEST RITUAL PROPORTION of any khipu analysed.
  Cross-colour actors: qaqa spans AB -> LC (geographic -> ritual); kaki spans AB -> GG (subject ->
  governance); chiqa spans LC -> AB (ritual -> subject).
narrative:
At Leymebamba, in the cloud forests of Chachapoyas, two khipus managed the relationship between sky and soil. UR006, the astronomical journal, recorded what the observers saw each month -- the Pleiades rising, Mars in opposition, eclipses darkening the moon. UR003, the agricultural calendar, translated those observations into action: which village (llaqa) would work which fields, where the terraces (qaqa, kaqa) were, when the corn (kaki) would be planted, how the communal labour (waki) would be organised.

The calendar descends five levels deep. At the top, production period. Below it, category label. Below that, detail. Below that, sub-detail. At the bottom, one single cord carries one individual task. The numbers come first; the words explain what was counted. This is a farmer's register, not a philosopher's notebook.

But the ritual colour lilac pervades: twenty-nine percent of all text labels are marked as ceremonial. In the Inca world, planting corn was as sacred as watching the stars. The same word -- kaki -- meant both. The Pleiades rose, the corn went into the ground. Sky and soil were one instrument.

The document has suffered. The primary cord has decomposed; fragile pendants were discarded. The knot directions were not recorded. The cataloguer wrote 'por falta del tiempo' -- for lack of time -- and moved on. The record is incomplete. But the structure holds: two hundred and eighty-one primary cords, most empty or numeric, and in the tree that hangs below them, twenty-one words that name what was counted. Terrain. Terraces. Corn. Walls. Boundaries. Labour. Villages. Frost. Harvest. Storage. Inspection. Huaca.

And between UR003 and its companion UR006, the Leymebamba pair tells the full story: observe the stars, then plan the agriculture, then assign the labour, then verify the results. Four khipus for four steps, two of them surviving. The Chachapoyas scribe Blas Valera, born here half a generation later, would describe exactly such a Pachaquipu -- the astronomical calendar khipu -- in his colonial-era writings. He was writing about what his ancestors had built.

prior_work.claim: Leymebamba (Chachapoyas, Amazonas) is the site of a major Late Horizon archive recovered from the Laguna de los Condores cliff-tombs, including the UR006 astronomical khipu (dated June 1473) and numerous associated administrative documents.
prior_work.citation: von Hagen, A. (2002). Chachapoya Iconography and Society at Laguna de los Condores, Peru; Centro Mallqui catalog.

prior_work.claim: Inca agriculture and astronomy were integrated: the Pleiades (kaki / Qollqa) heliacal rising opened the planting season for corn (also kaki), dark cloud constellations (qaqa) tracked rainy seasons, and the ceque system coordinated temples, farming, and star-watching as one system.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. (1981). At the Crossroads of the Earth and the Sky: An Andean Cosmology; Zuidema, R. T. (1964). The Ceque System of Cuzco.

prior_work.claim: Blas Valera (1544-1597), born in Chachapoyas, described the Pachaquipu (astronomical calendar khipu) in his colonial-era manuscripts -- matching the structure recovered in UR006 at Leymebamba, his own home region.
prior_work.citation: Valera, B. (c.1594). Quipu account in Hyland, S. (2011). Gods of the Andes: An Early Jesuit Account of Inca Religion.

v3_contribution.claim: RECLASSIFIED 2026-04 from astronomical_journal to agricultural_calendar: 12 of 13 words fit farming vocabulary vs 5 of 13 under pure astronomical reading. The sky/ground polysemy suppression rule points to the GROUND register given waki + llaqa + chiqa + waqa pattern + numbers-primary tree inversion. Classifier's prior astronomical reading is documented and superseded.

INVERTED TREE CONVENTION: ALL 21 STRING cords live on L2 or L3, NONE on L1. L1 = numbers (primary data); L2/L3 = text labels (annotations). Opposite convention from juridical khipus like UR176. Numbers come first; words explain what was counted. Hallmark of an AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION REGISTER.

evidence.test: L1-vs-subsidiary STRING distribution (0 on L1, 21 on L2-L3)

v3_contribution.claim: QAQA x7 as dominant terrain label (not dark constellation): the most frequent word in UR003. Under the agricultural-calendar reading qaqa = rocky terrain / terraces, consistent with the cloud-forest geography of Leymebamba where stone-retained terrace walls are the primary field infrastructure.

LILAC RITUAL COLOUR PROMINENT: LC (lilac) carries 6 of 21 STRING cords = 29 percent of text on a colour that makes up only 5 percent of the total palette. HIGHEST RITUAL-COLOUR PROPORTION of any khipu analysed -- consistent with Inca agriculture as a sacred practice.

evidence.test: LC STRING-fraction vs total LC-cord-fraction ratio

v3_contribution.claim: CROSS-COLOUR POLYSEMY ACTORS: qaqa spans AB -> LC (geographic terrain -> ritual terrace); kaki spans AB -> GG (corn subject -> corn governance); chiqa spans LC -> AB (ritual verification -> land inspection). The same word takes different roles by colour context.

evidence.test: cross-colour occurrences of qaqa, kaki, chiqa

v3_contribution.claim: LEYMEBAMBA PAIRING with UR006: UR003 and UR006 are the integrated sky-soil pair at the same site. UR006 = astronomical observation journal (what the sky shows); UR003 = agricultural calendar (what the community does in response). Blas Valera's Pachaquipu description (Chachapoyas-born) maps to exactly this pairing.

evidence.test: Leymebamba-Centro Mallqui co-provenance + Valera Pachaquipu textual anchor

v3_contribution.claim: PHYSICAL DAMAGE DOCUMENTED: primary cord decomposed, fragile pendants discarded, knot directions not recorded, recording incomplete (OKR Spanish note 'por falta del tiempo'). These loss factors explain 296 empty cords and the incomplete subsidiary tree; they do not undermine the 22 STRING readings at 95 percent dict hit.

companions.note: LEYMEBAMBA PAIR. UR006 = 874-cord astronomical observation journal (June 1473). UR003 = 769-cord agricultural calendar at the same site. Together they are the sky-soil integrated instrument that Blas Valera's Pachaquipu description maps to. Same museum (Centro Mallqui).

evidence.test: Centro Mallqui Leymebamba co-provenance + shared qaqa/kaki/maki/maqa vocabulary

companions.note: Chuquitanta workshop register (maki x15). Shares waki / maki / kiki labor vocabulary. UR003 is the agricultural-scale parallel (fieldwork) to UR177's workshop-scale labor.

Arica observation log (1831 cords, April 1453). Shares qaqa + kaki + waki + chiqa vocabulary. AS069 uses kaki as sky-register (Pleiades); UR003 uses kaki as ground-register (corn). The polysemy runs in opposite directions at the two sites.
Incahuasi commodity matrix (26 x 9). Shares qaqa + chaqa + kaqa cadastral vocabulary. UR003's qaqa = terraces; UR278's qaqa = commodity-row label. Different gloss contexts for the same root.
Chiastic labor corvee formulary. Shares waki / maki / kaki / paki vocabulary. UR051 uses them ritualistically (chant, incantation); UR003 uses them functionally (labor, rations, harvest).
Work register with kaki rations. UR087's kaki = rations for workers; UR003's kaki = the corn harvest itself + its Pleiades planting trigger. Upstream-downstream pair on the corn pipeline.

word_reading.note: 95 percent dict hit (21/22 STRING). All 13 distinct words are dictionary-stable. qaki remains uncertain (frost / dry). The sky/ground gloss context for qaqa and kaki is the main interpretive judgment, well-anchored on the agricultural-calendar structural signature.

document_type.note: agricultural_calendar supported by (a) 12/13 words fitting farming vocabulary, (b) inverted tree (numbers L1, words L2-L3), (c) 29 percent lilac ritual colour, (d) Leymebamba co-provenance with UR006, (e) Blas Valera's Pachaquipu description. The classifier's prior astronomical reading is superseded but the specific agricultural-calendar sub-type (sowing vs harvest vs full-cycle) is not resolved.

narrative.note: The sky-soil integrated reading is internally coherent and multiply anchored (Urton 1981 + Zuidema 1964 + Valera Pachaquipu + UR006 pairing). The agricultural-action-in-response-to-astronomical-signal is the best-fit interpretation. Specific production-period counts and subcategory detail require a full subsidiary-tree reading that the OKR partial catalogue does not support.

data_integrity.note: OKR documents primary-cord decomposition, discarded fragile pendants, and unrecorded knot directions. 38 percent empty cords mix document-design vacancies with damage. No Ascher layer summarised in the deep-read. The 22 STRING readings are robust; the 451 INT values and the 296 empties carry inherent loss.

sections.title: Inverted-Tree Convention
sections.reading: All 281 L1 cords carry numbers or empties -- NO TEXT on the main level. The primary cord is the production-period backbone. This is the structural signature of an AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION REGISTER: numbers first, words second.

key_cords.note: FIRST L1 -- opens the 281-cord numerical production-period backbone

sections.title: L2 Text Labels -- Farming Vocabulary
sections.reading: The subsidiary layer. 20 of the 21 STRING cords live here. qaqa x6 (rocky terrain / terraces, on AB geographic channel + LC ritual channel), kaki x2 (corn / Pleiades), waki x2 (communal labour), chiqa x2 (field inspection), plus paka, taqa, kaqa, waqa, maqa, maki, llaqa, kiki, qaki. The label layer that identifies what each L1 number counts.

key_cords.note: TERRACES -- dominant terrain label on geographic channel
CORN -- dual sky-ground doublet (planting signal + harvest)
INDIVIDUAL MIT'A WORKER on ritual channel
VILLAGE -- which community farms the terrace

sections.title: L3 Sub-Labels
sections.reading: The third level. Only 1 STRING: qaqa at L3, an additional terrain specification. Most L3 cords carry further numerical subdivision.

sections.title: L4-L5 Fine Details
sections.reading: 8 L4 cords + 1 L5 cord carry the finest-grain production detail. No text. The L5 cord is the document's deepest individual-task entry.

key_cords.note: DEEPEST ENTRY -- one individual task at L5

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
